title: salix × fragilis f. bullata
intro: Salix × fragilis f. bullata, also known as brittle willow or bullate brittle willow, is a hybrid species of willow tree that is a cross between Salix fragilis and another willow species. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bark of Salix × fragilis f. bullata contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which provides various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, and improving cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Salix × fragilis f. bullata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, although its use should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional.
